title: triaenophora integra
intro: Triaenophora integra, commonly known as "Cayratia," is a plant species belonging to the Cucurbitaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The plant's therapeutic actions include its potential to act as an anti-diabetic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant agent, which may contribute to its use in treating various health conditions. Bioactive constituents of Triaenophora integra include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which may be responsible for its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Triaenophora integra can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its high toxicity levels. Ingesting large quantities of the plant can lead to abdominal pain, fever, and respiratory issues, which may worsen in severe cases. Prolonged exposure to the plant's compounds can also cause liver and kidney damage, potentially resulting in long-term health complications. Furthermore, improper use of Triaenophora integra may interact with certain medications, exacerbating underlying health conditions and increasing the risk of adverse reactions.
